title: Kid-Friendly Sloppy Joes
description: A classic, comforting Sloppy Joes recipe that's quick to make and a hit with the whole family. Perfect for a busy weeknight meal, requiring minimal cleanup.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet or pot over medium heat.
Add ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it apart with a spoon. Drain any excess fat.
Stir in chopped onion, green bell pepper, and minced garlic. Cook until vegetables are softened, about 5-7 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together ketchup, Worcestershire sauce, brown sugar, mustard, and water.
Pour the sauce mixture over the beef and vegetables. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve hot on toasted hamburger buns.
tips: For an extra hidden veggie boost, finely grate carrots or zucchini into the mixture along with the onions and peppers.
Toast the hamburger buns lightly before serving to prevent them from getting too soggy.
Leftover Sloppy Joe mixture can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and reheated gently.
